question: [File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: SIXTY_SECONDS / THIRTY_ONE_MINUTES] Can an unprivileged external user craft timestamp filter parameters (filterKeys.TIMESTAMP) that span exactly the THIRTY_ONE_MINUTES boundary (1860n seconds in nanoseconds) to force the REST API into a timeout or partial-response state (httpStatusCodes.PARTIAL_CONTENT 206), causing downstream clients to treat stale/incomplete data as authoritative and creating a de-facto network partition for those clients?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: ONE_DAY_IN_NS] Can an unprivileged external user submit a filterKeys.TIMESTAMP query with a range spanning exactly ONE_DAY_IN_NS (86_400_000_000_000n nanoseconds) to trigger unbounded database scans that exhaust the DB connection pool, causing httpStatusCodes.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE (503) for all concurrent users and partitioning the network from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: MAX_LONG] Can an unprivileged external user supply filterKeys.TIMESTAMP=gte:9223372036854775807 (MAX_LONG = 2n**63n - 1n) to force a query against the maximum BigInt boundary, causing arithmetic overflow or unexpected DB behavior that results in httpStatusCodes.INTERNAL_ERROR (500) and effectively partitions the API from legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: MAX_INT32] Can an unprivileged external user set filterKeys.LIMIT to MAX_INT32 (2147483647) to trigger an unbounded result set fetch that exhausts Node.js heap memory, causing a process crash and complete service unavailability (httpStatusCodes.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE 503), partitioning all users from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: zeroRandomPageCostQueryHint] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a code path that injects the zeroRandomPageCostQueryHint ('set local random_page_cost = 0') into a session that is then reused by the connection pool, causing all subsequent queries in that pool connection to use zero random page cost and producing catastrophically slow sequential scans that degrade the entire service into a partition-like state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: NANOSECONDS_PER_MILLISECOND] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp value that, when multiplied by NANOSECONDS_PER_MILLISECOND (10n**6n), overflows the BigInt-to-integer conversion boundary used in DB queries, causing a malformed SQL parameter that triggers a DB error (httpStatusCodes.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE 503) and partitions the API?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: NANOS_PER_SECOND] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filterKeys.TIMESTAMP value that, when divided by NANOS_PER_SECOND (1_000_000_000n), produces a zero or negative epoch second, causing the API to query with an invalid time range that returns httpStatusCodes.PARTIAL_CONTENT (206) and misleads clients into believing the network is partitioned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: WEIBARS_TO_TINYBARS] Can an unprivileged external user supply a filterKeys.HBAR or balance filter value that, when multiplied by WEIBARS_TO_TINYBARS (10_000_000_000n), overflows the expected numeric range, causing a DB query with an out-of-range parameter that triggers httpStatusCodes.INTERNAL_ERROR (500) and partitions the API for all users querying balance endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: AUTO_RENEW_PERIOD_MULTIPLE] Can an unprivileged external user supply an auto-renew period value that, when multiplied by AUTO_RENEW_PERIOD_MULTIPLE (BigInt(1e9)), produces a value exceeding MAX_LONG (2n**63n-1n), causing a BigInt overflow in the REST layer that results in httpStatusCodes.INTERNAL_ERROR (500) and partitions the API?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Class: StatusCode / Method: isClientError()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response path where httpStatusCodes.BAD_GATEWAY (502) is returned but isClientError() returns false (since 502 >= 500), causing error-handling middleware to suppress the error message and log a misleading server error, masking a real upstream partition event and preventing operators from detecting the network split?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: httpStatusCodes.PARTIAL_CONTENT] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that consistently triggers httpStatusCodes.PARTIAL_CONTENT (206, message: 'Partial mirror node') by querying a timestamp range where only a subset of consensus nodes have submitted signatures, causing clients to cache and act on incomplete ledger state as if it were final, simulating a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: httpStatusCodes.isSuccess] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the httpStatusCodes.isSuccess lambda (code >= 200 && code < 300) by triggering a 206 PARTIAL_CONTENT response, which isSuccess() treats as successful, causing client-side logic to accept and cache partial mirror node data as complete, leading to a split-brain state between clients?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.TIMESTAMP with queryParamOperators] Can an unprivileged external user submit concurrent requests using filterKeys.TIMESTAMP with queryParamOperators.gte and queryParamOperators.lte set to the same nanosecond value, causing the DB to execute a zero-width range scan that returns empty results with httpStatusCodes.NO_CONTENT (204), which clients may misinterpret as a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.BLOCK_NUMBER / filterKeys.BLOCK_HASH] Can an unprivileged external user supply a filterKeys.BLOCK_NUMBER value of MAX_INT32 (2147483647) to force a query for a non-existent block, causing the API to return httpStatusCodes.NOT_FOUND (404) in a loop that exhausts rate-limit counters and triggers httpStatusCodes.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE (503) for legitimate users, partitioning them from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.NONCE] Can an unprivileged external user flood the API with requests using filterKeys.NONCE set to MAX_INT32 (2147483647) for non-existent transactions, causing repeated NOT_FOUND DB lookups that saturate the DB connection pool and produce httpStatusCodes.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE (503) for all users, effectively partitioning the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: ZERO_UINT256] Can an unprivileged external user supply ZERO_UINT256 ('0x000...000', 66 chars) as a filterKeys.BLOCK_HASH or filterKeys.TRANSACTION_HASH parameter, causing the API to perform a lookup for the all-zeros hash that may match a sentinel DB record or trigger an unhandled code path, resulting in httpStatusCodes.INTERNAL_ERROR (500)?
